10.title: Train GPT-2 (124M) from scratch in raw PyTorch

steps.title: Set up environment
steps.description: Verify your GPU is available and import the required libraries.
steps.hint: If CUDA is not available, check your PyTorch installation with: pip install torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121

steps.title: Define model architecture
steps.description: Implement a GPT-2 style transformer with token embeddings, positional embeddings, transformer blocks, and a language model head.
steps.hint: The Block class needs multi-head self-attention + MLP with GELU activation. Don't forget residual connections: x = x + attn(x).

steps.title: Write the training loop
steps.description: Standard training loop: forward pass → loss → backward → optimizer step. Log loss every 100 steps. Use AdamW with lr=3e-4.
steps.hint: Call optimizer.zero_grad() BEFORE loss.backward(). Use set_to_none=True for a small perf boost.

steps.title: Record your results
steps.description: Run training for 5,000+ steps. Record your final loss and throughput. Compare against the targets.
steps.hint: Loss should decrease to ~3.0–3.5 after 5K steps. If stuck above 4.0, check attention mask or positional embeddings.

checklist: Code ran and produced expected output
I can explain what loss.backward() does
I understand the role of each training loop step

starterCode.train.py:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.nn import functional as F
from model import GPT
import time

# ── Step 1: Set up environment ──
print(f'CUDA available: {torch.cuda.is_available()}')
device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'
print(f'Using device: {device}')

# Config
batch_size = 32
block_size = 256
max_steps = 5000
eval_interval = 100
learning_rate = 3e-4

# Load your dataset here
# text = open('data.txt').read()
# chars = sorted(set(text))
# vocab_size = len(chars)
vocab_size = 65  # placeholder

# ── Step 2: Create model ──
model = GPT(
    vocab_size=vocab_size,
    n_embd=384,
    n_head=6,
    n_layer=6,
    block_size=block_size,
).to(device)

print(f'Parameters: {sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())/1e6:.1f}M')

# ── Step 3: Training loop ──
optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=learning_rate)

for step in range(max_steps):
    # Get a batch (implement your data loader)
    # xb, yb = get_batch('train')
    xb = torch.randint(0, vocab_size, (batch_size, block_size), device=device)
    yb = torch.randint(0, vocab_size, (batch_size, block_size), device=device)

    # Forward
    logits, loss = model(xb, yb)

    # Backward
    optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()

    if step % eval_interval == 0:
        print(f'step {step}: loss {loss.item():.4f}')

# ── Step 4: Record results ──
print(f'\nFinal loss: {loss.item():.4f}')

# Measure throughput
torch.cuda.synchronize() if device == 'cuda' else None
t0 = time.time()
for _ in range(100):
    logits, loss = model(xb, yb)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
    optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
if device == 'cuda': torch.cuda.synchronize()
dt = time.time() - t0
tokens_per_sec = int(100 * xb.numel() / dt)
print(f'Throughput: {tokens_per_sec} tok/s')
starterCode.model.py:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.nn import functional as F
import math

# ── Step 2: Define model architecture ──

class Head(nn.Module):
    """Single head of self-attention."""
    def __init__(self, n_embd, head_size, block_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.key = nn.Linear(n_embd, head_size, bias=False)
        self.query = nn.Linear(n_embd, head_size, bias=False)
        self.value = nn.Linear(n_embd, head_size, bias=False)
        self.register_buffer('tril',
            torch.tril(torch.ones(block_size, block_size)))

    def forward(self, x):
        B, T, C = x.shape
        k = self.key(x)    # (B, T, head_size)
        q = self.query(x)  # (B, T, head_size)
        # Attention scores
        wei = q @ k.transpose(-2, -1) * (C ** -0.5)
        wei = wei.masked_fill(
            self.tril[:T, :T] == 0, float('-inf'))
        wei = F.softmax(wei, dim=-1)
        v = self.value(x)
        return wei @ v


class MultiHeadAttention(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_embd, n_head, block_size):
        super().__init__()
        head_size = n_embd // n_head
        self.heads = nn.ModuleList(
            [Head(n_embd, head_size, block_size)
             for _ in range(n_head)])
        self.proj = nn.Linear(n_embd, n_embd)

    def forward(self, x):
        out = torch.cat(
            [h(x) for h in self.heads], dim=-1)
        return self.proj(out)


class FeedForward(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_embd):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(n_embd, 4 * n_embd),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Linear(4 * n_embd, n_embd),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x)


class Block(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_embd, n_head, block_size=1024):
        super().__init__()
        self.sa = MultiHeadAttention(
            n_embd, n_head, block_size)
        self.ffwd = FeedForward(n_embd)
        self.ln1 = nn.LayerNorm(n_embd)
        self.ln2 = nn.LayerNorm(n_embd)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = x + self.sa(self.ln1(x))   # residual
        x = x + self.ffwd(self.ln2(x)) # residual
        return x


class GPT(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, n_embd=768, n_head=12,
                 n_layer=12, block_size=1024):
        super().__init__()
        self.block_size = block_size
        self.tok_emb = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, n_embd)
        self.pos_emb = nn.Embedding(block_size, n_embd)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            *[Block(n_embd, n_head, block_size)
              for _ in range(n_layer)])
        self.ln_f = nn.LayerNorm(n_embd)
        self.head = nn.Linear(n_embd, vocab_size, bias=False)

    def forward(self, idx, targets=None):
        B, T = idx.shape
        tok = self.tok_emb(idx)
        pos = self.pos_emb(
            torch.arange(T, device=idx.device))
        x = self.blocks(tok + pos)
        logits = self.head(self.ln_f(x))
        loss = None
        if targets is not None:
            loss = F.cross_entropy(
                logits.view(-1, logits.size(-1)),
                targets.view(-1))
        return logits, loss
